sn56.11:0.2: 2. Chapter on Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dhamma
sn56.11:0.3: Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dhamma
sn56.11:1.1: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Bārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana.
sn56.11:1.2: There the *Bhagavā* addressed the group of five *bhikkhus*:
sn56.11:10.1: But when my knowledge and vision as it truly is, with its three rounds and twelve aspects, concerning these four noble truths was fully purified, *bhikkhus*, then I claimed in the world with its gods, Māras, and Brahmās, among this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans, that I had awakened to the unsurpassed full awakening.
sn56.11:10.2: And knowledge and vision arose in me:
sn56.11:10.3: ‘My liberation is unshakable; this is the last birth; now there is no more renewed existence.’”
sn56.11:10.5: Delighted, the group of five *bhikkhus* rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
sn56.11:11.1: And while this explanation was being spoken, to the venerable Koṇḍañña there arose the dust-free, stainless eye of *dhamma* [*dhammacakkhuṁ*]:
sn56.11:11.2: “Whatever has the *dhamma* of arising [*samudayadhammaṁ*], all that has the *dhamma* of cessation [*nirodhadhammaṁ*].”
sn56.11:12.1: And when the wheel of *dhamma* had been set in motion by the *Bhagavā*, the earth-dwelling gods proclaimed the cry:
sn56.11:12.10: the Brahmā-host gods proclaimed the cry:
sn56.11:12.11: “At Bārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana, the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā*, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or Brahmā or anyone at all in the world.”
sn56.11:12.2: “At Bārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana, the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā*, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or Brahmā or anyone at all in the world.”
sn56.11:12.3: Hearing the cry of the earth-dwelling gods, the gods of the Four Great Kings proclaimed the cry:
sn56.11:12.4: “At Bārāṇasī, in the Deer Park at Isipatana, the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* has been set in motion by the *Bhagavā*, and it cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or Brahmā or anyone at all in the world.”
sn56.11:12.5: Hearing the cry of the gods of the Four Great Kings, the Tāvatiṁsa gods …
sn56.11:13.1: Thus at that moment, at that instant, at that second, the sound rose up as far as the Brahmā world.
sn56.11:13.2: And this ten-thousandfold world-system shook, trembled, and quaked, and an immeasurable, magnificent radiance appeared in the world, surpassing the divine majesty of the gods.
sn56.11:14.1: Then the *Bhagavā* uttered this inspired saying:
sn56.11:14.2: “Koṇḍañña has truly understood! Koṇḍañña has truly understood!”
sn56.11:14.3: That is how the venerable Koṇḍañña came to have the name “Aññāsikoṇḍañña.”
sn56.11:2.1: “These two extremes, *bhikkhus*, should not be practiced by one who has gone forth.
sn56.11:2.3: There is devotion to indulgence in sensual pleasures, which is low, vulgar, ordinary, not noble, and not beneficial; and there is devotion to self-mortification, which is painful, not noble, and not beneficial.
sn56.11:2.4: Without approaching either of these extremes, the middle way awakened to by the Realized One leads to vision, leads to knowledge, to peace, to direct knowledge, to full awakening, to *nibbāna*.
sn56.11:3.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is that middle way awakened to by the Realized One, which leads to vision, leads to knowledge, to peace, to direct knowledge, to full awakening, to *nibbāna*?
sn56.11:3.2: It is just this noble eightfold path, that is:
sn56.11:3.3: right view, right intention, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right *sati* [*sammāsati*], right *samādhi* [*sammāsamādhi*].
sn56.11:3.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that middle way awakened to by the Realized One, which leads to vision, leads to knowledge, to peace, to direct knowledge, to full awakening, to *nibbāna*.
sn56.11:4.1: This, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.2: birth is *dukkha*, aging is *dukkha*, illness is *dukkha*, death is *dukkha*, association with the unloved is *dukkha*, separation from the loved is *dukkha*, not getting what one wants is also *dukkha*—in brief, the five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*upādāna*] [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] are *dukkha*.
sn56.11:4.3: This, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the origin of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.4: it is this *taṇhā* that leads to renewed existence, accompanied by delight and passion, delighting here and there, that is:
sn56.11:4.6: This, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.7: the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, giving it up, relinquishing it, release, non-attachment.
sn56.11:4.8: This, *bhikkhus*, is the noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn56.11:4.9: it is just this noble eightfold path, that is:
sn56.11:5.1: “‘This is the noble truth of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:5.2: ‘This noble truth of *dukkha* is to be fully understood,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard … arose in me.
sn56.11:5.3: ‘This noble truth of *dukkha* has been fully understood,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:6.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the origin of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:6.2: ‘This noble truth of the origin of *dukkha* is to be abandoned,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard … arose in me.
sn56.11:6.3: ‘This noble truth of the origin of *dukkha* has been abandoned,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:7.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:7.2: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha* is to be directly realized,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard … arose in me.
sn56.11:7.3: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of *dukkha* has been directly realized,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:8.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha*,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:8.2: ‘This noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha* is to be developed [*bhāvetabbaṁ*],’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard … arose in me.
sn56.11:8.3: ‘This noble truth of the way of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha* has been developed [*bhāvitaṁ*],’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, vision arose in me, knowledge arose in me, discernment [*paññā*] arose in me, understanding arose in me, light arose in me.
sn56.11:9.1: As long as my knowledge and vision as it truly is, with its three rounds and twelve aspects, concerning these four noble truths was not fully purified, *bhikkhus*, I did not claim in the world with its gods, Māras, and Brahmās, among this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans, that I had awakened to the unsurpassed full awakening.
